title: Deal Pricing Lead - EMEA
content:
Who we are 
 About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the team 
 Within our GTM & Product Operation group, Deal Pricing is a dedicated function that provides deal pricing expertise, financial insights and deal support for negotiated users to Stripe’s global Go-to-Market teams. We work to ensure deals create economic value for Stripe by establishing the deal commercial framework for a scaling sales organization, actively supporting large and strategic deals and providing pricing insights and feedback to sales, finance and product leaders. Deal Pricing frequently works with cross-functional business partners across Operations, Products, Finance & Strategy, Partnership and there is constant collaboration with Deal Pricing colleagues across all regions. 
 What you’ll do 
 You will be managing a team of Deal pricing Strategists supporting our EMEA and APAC regions and be the representative of Deal Pricing in front of our regional leaders. Your team will be instrumental in providing thought leadership to regional sales teams and sales executives. 
 In addition to providing key financial insights and recommendations to business partners and Stripe leadership, you and your team will stretch beyond the boundaries of deal pricing operations by contributing to the development of improved  or tailored pricing framework processes, systems and governance and to help build and roll-out next-generation commercial constructs in order to support our various sales motions and users segments while mitigating risk to Stripe.  
 Responsibilities 
   
 
 Oversee and coach a team of Deal Pricing Strategists across EMEA and APAC, who are serving as key financial and strategic thought partners to Go-to-Market sales leader in both pricing deals at scale and pricing strategic or complex deals. 
 Partner with regional sales leaders and cross-functional partners to prioritise deal advisory and support and to refine pricing and commercial approaches and best practices. 
 Be “hands-on” by actively building sophisticated financial models, conducting pricing optimization, structuring complex deals and supporting the review and approval processes on key deals or segments  
 Be responsible for defining, refining and implementing our pricing and margin guidance and for teaming with our cross-functional partners to identify levers for financial uplift. 
 Help develop and refine new frameworks to evaluate deal’s attractiveness to Stripe, ensuring alignment between these frameworks, the company’s long range plan and mutually agreeable outcomes with cross-functional business partners. 
 Maintain up to date pricing benchmarks and contribute to team analytical design and production efforts, using this data to enhance our pricing philosophy and provide insights and recommendations to the GTM leaders you serve. 
 Help develop and roll-out enablement across EMEA and APAC on all pricing matters. 
 
 Who you are 
 We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. 
 Minimum requirements 
 
 Minimum 10 years of experience in finance or deal pricing at a company, or experience within investment banking, management consulting. 
 Minimum 5 years of experience in deal management ideally in SaaS or FinTech industry. 
 A proven ability to build collaborative working relationships with business partners through a combination of influencing and awareness. 
 The ability to deal effectively with ambiguity and thrive in an unstructured, fast-moving environment by driving business results without significant supervision. 
 Experience presenting findings and recommendations to senior leadership, preferably to sales of go-to-market business partners. 
 Excellent financial modeling, verbal and written communication skills. 
 Experience in people managing an international team. 
 
 Preferred requirements 
 
 Experience with deal operations at companies with subscription or consumption-based business models. 
 Understanding of the Payment Industry and its commercial considerations.

title: Deal Strategist
content:
Who we are  
 About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies - from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups - use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the team 
 Global Business Services’ (GBS) primary goal is to ensure operational excellence across deal cycles, increase revenue through the development of transformative commercial constructs, and drive closure of Stripe’s deal portfolio. The team's reach is widespread across Stripe, not exclusive to one sales segment or geographical region; GBS drives change across go-to-market, positively impacting Stripe users and cross-functional teams. GBS’s priorities are set through the direction of Stripe leadership and executive vision, partnership with sales and cross-functional organizations, as well as user feedback and market insights. 
 What you’ll do 
 Stripe’s Global Business Services (GBS) team is looking for a Deal Strategist to focus on the closure of custom and standard commercial deals. You will be responsible for deal lifecycle management and supporting cross-organizational alignment. This role requires a background in driving custom deals to closure, a strong understanding of pricing frameworks, and experience supporting sales through negotiations. 
 The candidate will have experience with SaaS products, pricing strategies, and a background in supporting selling motions. The candidate will have strong communication skills. You will love this job if you are a natural relationship and mechanism builder. 
 Responsibilities 
 
 Manage end-to-end customer deals —drafting, editing, and reviewing agreements—while supporting the sales cycle to ensure deal terms align with business goals, reflect strategic priorities, and close as forecasted. 
 Act as the primary point of contact between Sales, Legal, and other cross-functional stakeholders for deal-related questions, approvals, and escalations. 
 Work directly with sales teams, sales leaders, services/support teams and customers to advise on deal structures, establish compelling commercials, and drive closure of customer issues. 
 Advise sales teams on the best approach by understanding customer and partner requirements, while balancing custom terms with internal system constraints. 
 Remove internal blockers and drive solutions between cross-functional teams such as Product, Legal, Finance, Accounting, etc. 
 Support complex negotiations involving bespoke commercial and legal agreements. 
 Support the pricing and structuring of multi-threaded product use cases in all segments and industries especially in highly competitive sales. 
 Quickly build credibility and trust with internal customers. 
 Provide strong support, communication, and persuasion skills including XFN and senior leaders 
 
 Who you are 
 We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. 
 Minimum requirements 
 
 BA/BS degree in Business, Technology, Finance, or Economics. 
 6-8+ years of experience in deal strategy/procurement/pricing strategy/financial consulting/legal. 
 Experience working in ambiguous, quickly scaling environments, comfortable with high-agency. 
 Strong communication, presentation, negotiation, and project management skills. 
 Experience working in Payments and/or SaaS.

title: Design Engineer, Expansion
content:
Who we are  
 About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies - from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups - use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the team 
 Web Presence and Platform is organized into two pillars, each of which is grouped into pods that focus on the central tenets of Stripe's public mission. The Presence pillar creates industry-leading designs for Stripe’s front door surfaces, educating customers about the power of our platform, sharing Stripe’s ideas and expertise with the public, and driving adoption. The Platform pillar builds the internal machinery that powers these surfaces, and is responsible for making our websites fast, stable, and easy to update. 
 Together we design and build stripe.com and other sites that amount to what is, for many, their first impression of Stripe. As such, WPP offers exciting opportunities to have a major impact on Stripe’s success. We want to make every pixel count, we want it to be enthralling, and we want to help other Stripes seamlessly benefit from our systematic work. 
 What you’ll do 
 As a Design Engineer you will concept, prototype, and build new experiences for our web presence. You’ll build new tools and experiences to illustrate the value of Stripe’s products. You’ll often craft individual experiences or animations that distill a complex topic into a simple concept, such as  stripe.com/checkout . You’ll constantly consider in-browser performance, SEO and discoverability, and accessibility. You’ll both use existing components and create new ones for other designers and engineers to consume and build upon. 
 All Design Engineers in WPP have a shared commitment to user experience and craft, but each pod has subtly different focus areas. The Collective pod leverages design, animation, and interaction to drive awareness, educate prospective users, and share Stripe’s expertise across public surfaces such as  Stripe.com ,  Stripe Sessions ,  Stripe Press , and  Link . The Expansion pod brings the same creativity and executional rigor to attracting new prospects and driving conversion, focusing on user journeys, interactive and highly polished tools for users, and building targeted experiences for our global users. 
 As a Design Engineer on the Expansion pod, you will bring a high standard for creativity and execution to a variety of high-impact opportunities. You will reimagine the user experience moments that contribute to acquisition journeys. You will build delightful and original interactive tools and resources that attract users. You will collaborate deeply across design, engineering, and cross-functional teams and work across teams and tech stacks to create new site capabilities.  
 Responsibilities 
 
 Collaborate with our team’s designers and partners to prototype, implement, and maintain dynamic interactive tools and experiences 
 Work closely with other design engineers, pairing, mentoring, and continually building a culture of excellence 
 Maintain and extend our design systems 
 Build high-polish web pages that meet high standards for SEO, accessibility, and performance 
 
 Who you are 
 We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. 
 Minimum requirements 
 
 5+ years of relevant web front-end development or creative technology experience at a tech-forward, product-driven company 
 ​​Advanced knowledge of modern HTML and CSS 
 Experience writing clear, elegant code in a team environment 
 A zeal for the web as a storytelling medium, demonstrable through a portfolio of design work and frontend code showcasing design systems, engaging microsites, Figma plugins, webGL experiments, CSS art, or anything else—you tell us 
 Experience designing and building public facing websites that work elegantly across commonly used browsers and devices 
 
 Preferred qualifications 
 
 A good understanding of the formal elements of design, not just limited to the web and including typography, layout, balance, and proportion 
 Experience building highly complex interactive tools, including state management and data fetching 
 Proficiency with React, with a focus on interactions, animation, performance, and polish 
 Experience with A/B testing, synthetic monitoring, or accessibility testing

title: Design Recruiter, AMER
content:
About Stripe 
 Stripe is a technology company that builds economic infrastructure for the internet. Businesses of all sizes—from new startups to public companies like Amazon and Salesforce—use our software to accept online payments and run technically sophisticated financial operations in more than 100 countries. By helping businesses accept payments from anywhere in the world and accelerating the economic shift from offline to online, we aim to increase the GDP of the internet.  
 What you’ll do 
 To further this important mission, we are looking for a driven and collaborative Design Recruiter who has experience with design recruiting to grow and scale our global design organization. You can expect high impact and wide-ranging responsibility: owning full-cycle recruiting on niche searches, contributing to our geo strategy with talent market insights, and delivering best-in-class candidate experience. This person must be a builder who thrives in a learning environment—unafraid to try new things, embrace new ideas, navigate ambiguity, and welcome suggestions for how we can iterate on our processes. 
 Responsibilities: 
 
 Partner with senior leaders across the design organization to understand hiring objectives and develop recruiting strategies to identify top talent across various skillsets including product design, brand design, content design, design engineering, UX research, and more.  
 Help initiate creative and scalable changes to our existing recruiting processes. 
 Track and analyze metrics and report on hiring progress to leadership on a regular cadence. 
 Focus on creating strategic plans to attract and engage specialized tech talent across different geos. 
 Work closely with cross-functional teams like Finance, People Ops, Benefits, Onboarding and Legal to deliver a high-quality and holistic candidate experience. 
 
 Minimum requirements: 
 
 5+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience with 2+ years of Design Recruiting experience in a fast-paced, high-growth environment, including in house experience. 
 Experience building high performing, high craft design teams and delivering an exceptional candidate experience. 
 A growth mindset and truly flexible. You can shift tasks and priorities when circumstances change (at a fast-growing startup, they often do). 
 An active and data driven approach to problem solving; you enjoy making sense of the trends and using the insights to better your process to make it more efficient. 
 Proven abilities to attract passive senior candidates and build a diverse pipelines across different hiring locations. 
 Strong operational rigor with the ability to stay organized amidst change. 
 Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with an appropriate blend of professionalism and friendliness.

title: Developer Experience Engineer, Privy
content:
Who we are 
 About Privy 
 Our mission is to make privacy and user ownership the default online. We build simple, flexible developer tooling that make it easy to build products that put users first. By leveraging modern cryptography, we shift the status quo around digital ownership and protect the accounts and assets of millions of users. 
 Engineering at Privy is not just about writing world-class code. We create software that turns complex technical systems into delightful developer tools. We assemble tried-and-true primitives into intuitive, responsive APIs and beautiful interfaces. We believe in open-source work and transparency with our teammates and users. We encourage each other to think big, run experiments and follow our curiosity so we can build better tooling that lets developers shine and empower their users. 
 Learn more about Privy:  Privy and Stripe: Bringing crypto to everyone 
 What you’ll do 
 As our first Developer Experience Engineer hire at Privy, you will bridge the gaps between our product and developer community. You will be responsible for improving developer experience, reducing support burden through proactive education, and building developer advocacy initiatives to drive adoption and engagement of our product. Customer obsession is a core part of Privy’s culture. You’ll work closely with fellow engineers, product, and business teams to ensure Privy's customers needs are met.  
 Responsibilities 
 
 Create and maintain code samples, starter repos, and recipes that help developers integrate our product effectively 
 Establish and create a regular cadence of developer-focused content (blog posts, video tutorials, code samples) 
 Collaborate with internal teams to organize our documentation, prioritize content gaps and improvements, and gather developer feedback on all content effectiveness. 
 Engage with the developer community through industry events, hackathons, and other channels.  
 
 Who you are 
 Minimum requirements 
 
 6+ years of experience in a developer advocacy, technical evangelism, technical writing or similar role. 
 Adept programming skills and a deep understanding of the technologies the company uses.  
 Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain complex concepts in a clear and concise manner.  
 Passion for building and engaging with developer communities.  
 Strong problem solving skills, identify and solve technical challenges.  
 Ability to build relationships, collaborate with others, and work effectively in a team environment.  
 
 Preferred qualifications 
 
 Strong preference for experience in an API-based business and in payments, fintech, or crypto.

title: EMEA ABM and Executive Marketing Manager
content:
Who we are 
 About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world's largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the team 
 The Stripe EMEA Marketing team is a dynamic and rapidly growing group dedicated to transforming customers into enthusiastic advocates for Stripe. Collaborating closely with sales, we focus on accelerating the path to purchase and enhancing product adoption among our user base. Our regional marketing efforts involve crafting and executing tailored marketing plans that resonate with local audiences, as well as spearheading our most significant conferences and sponsorships that drive go-to-market (GTM) success and business growth. Join us and be part of a culture that celebrates teamwork, innovates boldly, and makes a meaningful impact. 
 What you’ll do 
 We’re looking for a strategic, creative, and results-oriented marketing leader to define and drive our Account-Based Marketing (ABM) and Executive Engagement strategy throughout EMEA.  
   
 This is a senior individual contributor role for someone who thrives at the intersection of strategy, execution, and cross-functional influence. You will translate global priorities into locally resonant programs, ensuring that Stripe’s most strategic audiences have high-quality, insight-driven experiences throughout their lifecycle, from early exploration to long-term partnership.  
   
 Working with regional marketing managers, you will be instrumental in building the programs and operations to engage Executives and prospects with compelling experiences and content to create and nurture sales pipeline. The ideal candidate is data-driven and has an unbridled passion for working hand-in-hand with regional marketing/sales teams to drive successful user outcomes. 
   
 Responsibilities 
 
 Craft the executive marketing strategy within high-priority account segments, including established enterprises and digital native businesses. 
 Define and execute high-touch programs and executive engagement plans, including virtual and in-person field events designed for senior personas. 
 Guide regional marketers on ABM frameworks and best practice that boost awareness and adoption of Stripe. 
 Oversee the development of persona based mid- and lower-funnel regional marketing programs to accelerate lead conversion and generate pipeline. 
 Partner with Demand Generation, Product Marketing, and Partnerships to identify, scope, and produce localized content and marketing assets. 
 Track, measure, and communicate program results, continually optimizing for efficiency and scale while fostering a culture of innovation. 
 Be creative in finding new ways to advise a diverse range of enterprise personas about the value Stripe brings to businesses. 
 
 Who you are 
 We’re looking for an experienced, creative, and results-oriented leader who understands the diverse needs of businesses and can effectively collaborate with sales leaders to translate business needs into engagement opportunities. 
 Minimum requirements 
 
 10+ years of relevant B2B marketing experience, including specific experience with Account-Based Marketing (ABM), field marketing, and multi-touch campaigns. 
 Proven track record of defining and executing marketing strategies that deliver against funnel metrics, KPIs, and pipeline contribution. 
 Strong communication skills, including the ability to convey complex topics with simplicity and present business insights in an executive-ready format. 
 Strategic mindset with strong analytical capabilities and a data-driven approach to decision-making. 
 Deep experience collaborating with Sales teams on joint planning and execution, establishing trust as a strategic partner. 
 Experience driving effective ABM programs with measurable results. 
 
 Preferred qualifications 
 
 Experience in the payments, financial technology, or adjacent industries, or marketing FinTech solutions or to FinTech companies. 
 A startup mentality with a bias to action and the ability to flex in a fast-paced environment. 
 Understanding of the payments and ecommerce industries and their partner ecosystems. 
 Demonstrated success delivering marketing programs with and through consulting and technology partners.

title: Employment Counsel
Engineering Manager - Collaboration Tools
content:
Who we are 
 About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the team 
 The Collaboration team helps Stripe employees work smarter, find information, share knowledge, get to know each other, and request and receive help from one another.  Many of our solutions are integrated into Stripe Home, our custom-built intranet (read more about it here:  (https://stripe.com/blog/stripe-home ).  
 The team is very nimble and works across a wide stack: on any given day, we discuss user interface design, data models, API design, infrastructure maintenance, search relevance/GenAI, or all of the above. We're excited to innovate in a space not traditionally thought of as being at the forefront of innovation. 
 As a team that builds critical, company-wide tools, we prioritize projects with the highest leverage. This involves extensive cross-functional collaboration, partnering with groups ranging from engineering to HR to top management. One of the greatest rewards is seeing the direct impact our work has on our users: our fellow Stripe employees. 
 What you’ll do 
 As an engineering manager on Stripe’s Collaboration team, you will be a key member of a team that acts as a force multiplier on the internal tools that Stripe uses. You will focus on building innovative GenAI-powered experiences that help Stripe employees be more productive across various tasks and activities. You will build these experiences by collaborating with multiple teams across Stripe and experimenting with different ideas and the newest technologies. 
 This is a new team being staffed out of Dublin, and aligned with an existing peer US team. Over time, it will own tools, products, and services end-to-end, as well as platform pieces that the broader Collaboration team builds and maintains. 
 Responsibilities 
 
 Recruit top engineering talent in partnership with Stripe’s recruiting team to build a diverse and high-performing team 
 Scale the team up from zero, and work with US-based teams to align charter and ownership, and take over more domains as the team scales 
 Build full-stack solutions across variety of Stripe's internal tools, including delightful web applications, Chrome extensions, GenAI-powered features, and integrations with other internal tools and platforms 
 Engage directly with users to understand their business needs and translate requirements into concrete solutions 
 Collaborate with cross-functional teams including product, design, and other engineering teams to deliver these solutions 
 Push our vision for the internal tools space to be the best-in-the-industry powered by the latest technologies and convert it to real productivity gains for Stripe employees 
 Lead engineering and design processes, and coaching, mentoring, and supporting your team as they build innovative, user-facing solutions 
 
 Who you are 
 We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. 
 Minimum requirements 
 
 You love to build delightful user experiences 
 5+ years of engineering management experience, directly managing and growing teams of 5+ engineers focused on building and shipping products at scale 
 10+ years of full time software development experience, with a strong background in full-stack development 
 Experience hiring and building new teams from zero 
 Experience leading teams that have shipped and maintained critical user-facing products 
 Strong technical proficiency to engage thoughtfully with engineers about architecture and product design, with a particular focus on modern web technologies, performance, and user experience 
 Experience building extensible, leveraged software solutions that scale to different kinds of user needs, with the ability to empathize with users and advocate for exceptional user experiences 
 
 Preferred qualifications 
 
 You have a passion for enabling people to do their best work 
 You have experience working across geographies and with North America-based teams 
 You have industry experience working with Ruby, TypeScript, and React 
 You have a healthy respect for corporate software, understanding where it shines and where it could be improved 
 You enjoy working together with your team toward common goals, but are able to drive your work forward autonomously 
 You work very well cross-functionally, and are able to think rigorously and make hard decisions and tradeoffs

title: Engineering Manager, Core Events
content:
About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies-from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups-use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the Team 
 We build and operate Stripe’s Event Infrastructure — the reliable, low-latency backbone that captures, routes, and persists billions of events daily. We design resilient, scalable systems for real-time ingestion, filtering, transformation, and high-throughput delivery to downstream consumers. We prioritize observability, backpressure control, and graceful degradation to keep payments and platform features running under any load. By automating operations and iterating on capacity and cost optimizations, we enable product teams to build on a predictable event-streaming foundation. 
 The Impact You’ll Have 
 As an Engineering Manager in the infrastructure organization, you are expected to see problems others don't and rally people to independently create solutions. You will move beyond day-to-day management to act as an industry leader, effectively advocating for your organization's mission, values, and impact. 
 
 Architect the Org & Culture:  Design and scale a high-leverage org structure. You will define what "Elite Engineering" looks like, setting the bar for craftsmanship and operational rigor serving as a blueprint for Stripe infrastructure. 
 Vision and Strategy:  Define the scope, vision, and goals for your organization with little or no guidance from your manager. You will anticipate industry trends and use them to influence Stripe's long-range plans, setting direction on a multi-year timeframe for Stripe’s online storage layer. 
 Drive Operational Excellence:  You will instill a culture of rigorous thinking and meticulous craftsmanship, ensuring our infrastructure remains proactive and resilient as Stripe’s business demands evolve. 
 Bridge Engineering & Business:  You will collaborate with cross-functional leaders to align deep engineering needs with global business growth forecasts. 
 Build the Future Team:  Proactively invest in the development of the organization and its people at all levels. You will obsess over talent, hiring and coaching other managers to elevate the skills of the leadership team. 
 
 Who You Are 
 We are looking for a visionary leader who thrives on operating Tier0 infrastructure and building world-class teams. You should be equally comfortable in high-level strategic conversations and deep-diving into database internals. 
 Minimum Requirements: 
 
 Proven Leadership:  Minimum 5 years of experience in a technical engineering management role; overseeing strategic projects. 
 Systems Expertise:  A deep technical background in distributed systems and experience operating Tier0 infrastructure at massive scale. 
 Product Mindset:  Ability to bridge deep engineering needs with global business growth forecasts and product strategy, specifically building abstractions that empower thousands of other developers. 
 Strategic Navigator:  Experience managing through ambiguity and making critical trade-offs between reliability, efficiency, and long-term architectural health. 
 Curiosity and Humility : A deep curiosity about the "nuts-and-bolts" of the business (e.g., global money movement) combined with the humility to continue learning and growing as a leader 
 Communication:  Exceptional written and verbal communication skills. 
 
 Preferred Qualifications: 
 
 Scale and Infrastructure:  Successfully shipped and operated critical infrastructure (database internals or related field) with significant responsibility over funds and/or critical data 
 Strategic Ambiguity:  Ability to navigate complex, high-impact work where you must define your own scope and vision 
 
 Cross-Functional Influence:  A track record of getting other teams on board with your vision to support execution in a way that benefits the broader company

title: Engineering Manager, Data Engineering
content:
Who we are 
 About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the team 
 We are experts in data, working to make it cost-effective, understandable, and trustworthy. We build pipelines processing billions of events a day and are stewards of canonical data warehouses and datasets delivering products for Stripe Users while embedding with teams to build their data products.  We are experts in using the Stripe Data Platform and to scale we lead the data culture and data education to enable product teams to own their data. We invest in [AI] Data Ops to scale incident handling and serve as an escalation path for data incidents to minimize their impact. The Data Engineering Solutions team will work closely with product teams delivering trustworthy data. 
 What you’ll do 
 We are looking for a hands-on Tech lead Manager to architect, build, and maintain the core data infrastructure powering Stripes business operations. This person will set the technical direction and lead a high-impact team working across Treasury, Finance & Billing. The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in modern data systems including Airflow/Spark/Kafka/Flink with the ability to partner closely with business stakeholders, shape team culture and unlock new capabilities. You will:

content:

 About the team 
 Link  helps Stripe’s merchants deliver frictionless and secure checkout experiences by auto-filling customers’ payment information. Link enables customers to checkout in just 6 seconds, increasing conversion rates for Stripe’s merchants by over 7%. Link is available on the sites of hundreds of thousands of merchants—customers can save payment info on any Link-supported site, and pay in one click across the Link merchant network. Link is built into merchants’ existing payments integrations on Stripe, with no additional charges or fees. 
 What you’ll do 
 We're hiring an Engineering Manager to work on Link’s Payments team. Millions of consumers already use Link every year, and our goal is to build even more flexibility by offering consumers the ability to pay with their desired payment methods when they transact with Link, across all Stripe merchants, regardless of geography or business model. 
 Responsibilities 
 Drive Product Innovation:  Build novel Link payment capabilities designed to move the needle on conversion for businesses and create seamless value for consumers. 
 Navigate Product-Market Fit:  Lead the team through rapid iteration to find product-market fit, building a diverse suite of products both at the point of checkout and in adjacent consumer spaces. 
 Full-Stack Ownership:  Enable your team to work across a wide range of systems, processes, and technologies, empowering them to own and solve complex problems from end-to-end. 
 Operational Excellence:  Design and lead engineering processes that allow the team to do its best work while engaging effectively with the broader Stripe ecosystem. 
 Cross-Functional Partnership:  Collaborate deeply with stakeholders across Product, Design, Data Science, Operations, Partnerships, and Marketing to align on strategy and execution. 
 Team Growth & Mentorship:  Actively develop the engineers on your team, providing the coaching and opportunities they need to advance in their careers. 
 Strategic Recruiting:  Partner with Stripe’s recruiting team to identify and hire world-class engineering talent to scale the Link organization. 
 Who you are 
 Minimum requirements 
 5+ years of experience  directly managing and growing software engineering teams. 
 Proven track record  of managing teams of 8+ engineers focused on shipping high-scale, consumer-facing, or infrastructure products. 
 Technical Proficiency:  Ability to engage deeply with engineers on architecture, system design, and technical trade-offs across the stack. 
 User-First Mindset:  A strong sense of empathy for the consumer and a drive to advocate for exceptional, low-friction user experiences. 
 Operational Excellence:  Experience thriving in a fast-paced, autonomous environment, managing multiple high-impact workstreams simultaneously. 
 Preferred qualifications 
 Experience with financial product companies and interest in financial products and markets 
 Productively lead fully remote teams 
 Experience managing mobile teams, shipping both production apps and SDKs 
 Hybrid work at Stripe 
 This role is available either in an office or a remote location (35+ miles or 56+ km from a Stripe office). 
 In-office expectations:  Office-assigned Stripes spend at least 50% of the time in their local office to balance in-person collaboration with personal flexibility. 
 Working remotely:  Remote Stripes work regularly from home. Stripe does not cover relocation costs to remote locations.

title: Engineering Manager - Onboarding Journeys
content:
About the team 
 The Onboarding Journeys team builds onboarding flows from some of Stripe’s highest-leverage use cases. The default onboarding flow covers our core journeys well, but we need more focused flows for workflows like cross-border onboarding, onboarding for publicly traded companies and government entities, and onboarding a new account into Organizations. This team identifies opportunities to build reusable onboarding flows for high-leverage segments, and then it designs and builds those flows end-to-end. 
 This is a pivotal moment for Onboarding, because as Stripe’s scope has grown, so has Onboarding’s scope. Onboarding now means navigating KYC forms, fraud and credit risk flows, supportability checks, and more. We’re building brand new AI-powered experiences to reinvent onboarding, turning it from a series of forms into a product in its own right. Our goal is to build the master key to unlock access to the world’s best financial services, and set a new bar for the industry on how good onboarding can be. 
 What you’ll do 
 Responsibilities 
 
 Be the editor-in-chief of the team’s plans, execution, and ultimate output 
 Work tightly with designers, product managers, engineering leaders, and cross-team partners to build and continually refine the team’s strategy and execution 
 Build a team culture of high agency, fast execution, and the psychological safety to always have “the real conversation” 
 Define day-to-day processes that set the team up for success in planning and execution 
 Learn from users often — sometimes by speaking with them directly and sometimes by metrics and indirect means — distill takeaways, and continually bring a deep awareness of user experience into the roadmap 
 Recruit and develop excellent engineers and coach them in doing the best work of their careers so far 
 
 Who you are 
 We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. 
 Minimum requirements 
 
 Experience successfully managing teams of 4+ engineers through career growth and multiple large projects, or experience as a senior engineer on multiple large projects involving 3+ engineers and heavy cross-team collaboration 
 Exceptional ability for cross-team collaboration, managing escalations, and shaping an impactful roadmap in an environment with multiple competing demands on your team’s time 
 Deeply product-oriented mindset with a track record leading the development of polished, user-centric features and interfaces. Successful candidates will be comfortable thinking like a product manager as well as an engineering manager. 
 Evidence of unusually high interest in user experience, and demonstrated ability to convert that interest into effective production features at scale 
 
 Preferred 
 
 Experience speaking directly with users and converting your takeaways into user research or product requirements documents 
 Experience managing geographically distributed teams

title: Engineering Manager, Product and Business
content:
Who we are 
 About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the Organization 
 At Stripe, our engineering organization is structured into several key divisions to maximize impact: 
 
 Core Engineering:  This division consists of three primary areas -  Application   and   Revenue   Platform ,  Infrastructure , and  Finance   Systems . The Application and Revenue Platform builds scalable infrastructure for innovative applications and global transaction processing. The Infrastructure division enhances the development, deployment, and operation of services across specialized platforms. Lastly, Finance Systems facilitates effective financial decision-making through reliable data and automation across multiple finance functions. 
 Product and Business Engineering:  This organization encompasses critical areas such as  Money as a Service (MaaS), Payments and Risk, Developer Experience and Product Platform (DEeP),  and  Revenue and Financial Automation (RFA) . These teams manage Stripe's core financial products, develop tools for efficient payment acceptance and risk management, provide a consistent developer experience, and create solutions that empower businesses to optimize their revenue operations. 
 
 What you’ll do 
 As an Engineering Manager at Stripe, you will play a pivotal role in driving the success of your team and the broader organization. Your responsibilities will include: 
 Responsibilities 
 
 Leading engineering and design processes, coaching, mentoring, and supporting your team as they build innovative solutions. 
 Collaborating with stakeholders across product, design, infrastructure, marketing, and operations to ensure alignment on projects and objectives. 
 Owning problems from end-to-end by managing diverse systems, processes, and technologies, while continuously seeking opportunities to optimize efficiency and user experience. 
 Upholding high engineering standards, ensuring consistency across codebases, and implementing best practices throughout your team. 
 Recruiting top engineering talent in partnership with Stripe’s recruiting team to build a diverse and high-performing team. 
 Supporting your team members' career growth by providing insights and development opportunities tailored to their goals. 
 
 Who you are 
 We're looking for a candidate who meets the minimum qualifications below, with preferred qualifications that would be advantageous but are not required. If you meet the minimum requirement, we encourage you to apply. 
 Minimum Requirements 
 
 4+ years of experience managing and growing teams of engineers. 
 Proven track record managing a team of 5+ software engineers focused on building and shipping products at scale. 
 An ability to empathize with users and advocate for exceptional user experiences. 
 Strong problem-solving skills, with a history of creatively tackling complex challenges. 
 Experience thriving in cross-functional environments, demonstrating rigorous thinking and effective decision-making. 
 Background in dealing with ambiguity and executing on multiple high-impact work streams simultaneously. 
 Commitment to fostering a supportive, feedback-driven, and challenging work environment. 
 Technical proficiency to engage thoughtfully with engineers about architecture and product design, offering valuable insights when necessary. 
 A mindset of autonomy and entrepreneurship, where you're excited to take responsibility and drive initiatives forward. 
 
 Alignment with Stripe’s Operating Principles 
 In this role, you will embody our  operating principles  by maintaining a focus on user outcomes, fostering open communication and cross-team collaboration, and embracing an iterative approach to both product development and team management. You will make decisions that reflect our commitment to integrity, excellence, and the long-term success of our users and the company. 
 If you are passionate about empowering engineers and driving impactful projects in a dynamic environment, we would love to hear from you!

User Billing provides a central entry point for Stripe teams to monetize products in global markets. We build platforms and services to define and manage pricing, calculate and collect Stripe’s revenue, and empower Stripe and our users to understand and manage revenue and costs. We work on customer-facing experiences, internal platforms, and data pipelines to empower Accounting, Finance, Strategy, Sales, and Stripe’s Products to drive business value across the entire user and product lifecycle.

 We’re looking for a seasoned technical leader to drive the technical direction and strategy for the platform that powers monetization for Stripe’s entire product suite, including APIs and tools for internal users and teams, backend systems, and data pipelines. You’ll work with leaders across product, engineering, sales, and many of our back office functions -finance and strategy, accounting, tax, and compliance - to influence strategy across the company. 
 User Billing is a core engine powering many of Stripe’s biggest bets, from shipping new products faster to redefining our commercial strategy. The right engineering leadership will allow us to scale more quickly and more reliably as our business accelerates. If that sounds exciting, we’d love to speak with you.

Set strategies and priorities to balance improvements across multiple dimensions including monetizing new products, enabling greater self-service and improved developer experience, and improving technical foundations and operational excellence. 
 Lead engineering and design processes, coaching, mentoring, and supporting your team as they build innovative solutions.

 Successfully recruited, mentored, and built high-performing teams, creating an inclusive environment that attracts diverse talent and empowers individuals to thrive.

title: Enterprise Risk Management Specialist, Luxembourg
content:
Position Overview:  Bridge Building S.A. (BBSA) is building a Luxembourg regulated EMI and CASP. We are seeking an Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) Specialist to build and operate BBSA’s enterprise risk framework across financial and non-financial risks, ensuring clear governance, effective risk assessments, and inspection-ready documentation. The role supports senior management and the Board with actionable risk reporting and disciplined issue remediation. 
 Key Responsibilities: 
 
 ERM Framework and Governance: 
 
 Implement and maintain BBSA’s ERM framework, including risk taxonomy, risk appetite statements, risk register, and escalation processes. 
 Coordinate risk governance forums and prepare management and Board risk reporting packs (KRIs/KPIs, trends, and key decisions). 
 
 Risk and Control Assessments: 
 
 Facilitate periodic risk and control assessments across BBSA functions (Operations, Treasury, Compliance/AML, ICT, Outsourcing). 
 Support business change and new product risk assessments, ensuring risks are identified, mitigated, and documented. 
 
 Issue, Incident, and Remediation Management: 
 
 Operate the issue management lifecycle: logging, root cause analysis, action plans, tracking, and validation of closure. 
 Partner with InfoSec and Compliance on operational incidents, control weaknesses, and regulatory findings. 
 
 Third Party and Outsourcing Risk: 
 
 Support outsourcing risk oversight, including concentration risk considerations, critical provider monitoring, and exit planning in collaboration with the Outsourcing function. 
 Ensure third party risk is consistently assessed and reported within the ERM framework. 
 
 Risk Culture and Continuous Improvement: 
 
 Contribute to risk policies, procedures, and training; promote risk awareness and pragmatic control ownership across the business. 
 Drive continuous improvement of risk metrics, reporting quality, and governance effectiveness. 
 
 
 Key Requirements: 
 Education: 
 
 Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Risk Management, Finance, Business, or a related field. 
 
 Experience: 
 
 5+ years in enterprise/operational risk management within regulated financial services. 
 Experience with payments/EMI/PI and/or crypto/fintech risk environments is a strong advantage. 
 
 Skills: 
 
 Strong facilitation and stakeholder management, with the ability to challenge constructively and drive outcomes.

title: Enterprise Services Manager
content:
Who we are 
 About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies - from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups - use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the team 
 Stripe’s Professional Services Sales team works with our most strategic users; You will help customers accelerate their growth and adoption on Stripe. This role is responsible for leading strategic conversations during pre-sales that will transform the payments and financial infrastructure for leading enterprises, ultimately driving revenue growth through our professional service offerings. 
 What you’ll do 
 As an Enterprise Services Manager, you will sell large Professional Services engagements to strategic clients and drive Stripe’s future growth engine by building relationships with prospective users and turning them into happy Stripe users. 
 Responsibilities 
 
 Own the entire Professional Services sales cycle for enterprise companies. 
 Partner with Account Executives on named account lists to develop account plans aimed at winning and expanding Professional Services business with enterprise clients. 
 Drive the success of Professional Services by managing and forecasting bookings. 
 Craft custom proposals that align with clients' business outcomes, utilizing services across Professional Services and partner offerings. 
 Advise senior leadership in customer organizations on building business cases for digital transformation. 
 Lead pre-sales workshops with customers, focusing on strategy, vision, discovery, and assessment. 
 Shape Professional Services deals, and build and negotiate contracts/Statements of Work. 
 Engage with Product and Engineering teams to influence product strategy for large clients. 
 Collaborate effectively with GTM partners to integrate complementary strategies and drive mutual growth objectives. 
 Prepare the delivery team with user insights to ensure that the delivery aligns with the desired business outcomes. 
 
 Who you are 
 We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. 
 Minimum requirements 
 
 5+ years of professional services sales experience, preferably at a technology company or a consulting firm, with a track record of top performance. 
 Proven experience in pre-sales and sales of sizable service engagements, as well as in contract and Statement of Work (SOW) development. 
 Demonstrated ability to lead large transformational engagements using disruptive technologies. 
 Thrives on the urgency and importance of working with our largest customers, achieving set objectives while balancing competing priorities. 
 Ability to understand complex business requirements and develop tailored solutions. 
 Demonstrated ability to navigate ambiguity to produce strategic insights and identify opportunities. 
 
 Preferred qualifications 
 
 Knowledge of the sales cycles associated with a SaaS product or technology platform for strategic enterprise customers. 
 An in-depth understanding of the buyer journey and the ability to lead complex, multi-party sales in a highly consultative manner. 
 Experience working with upper middle market and enterprise companies. 
 A proven track record of driving cross-functional alignment among a broad set of stakeholders, including engineering, sales, support, product, marketing, data science, and senior leadership, to achieve growth targets. 
 Experience in building and scaling a go-to-market (GTM) growth discipline, including the development of strategy, processes, and operating models. 
 Proven ability to create a strong growth culture within a team. 
 Background in a growth-stage internet/software company and/or the payments industry. 
 Familiarity with APIs and their role in digital solutions.

title: Events Producer, Expo and Stripe Tour
content:
Who we are  
 About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies - from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups - use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the team 
 We build impactful experiences that drive awareness of Stripe, educate users and prospects on Stripe solutions, foster community, and deliver high quality leads to our sales team. 
 What you’ll do 
 We are seeking a highly organized, detail-oriented, and creative producer to support the planning and execution of physical experiences for Stripe Sessions and Stripe Tour, with a strong focus on our expo hall. This role is pivotal in concepting and delivering engaging experiences that showcase Stripe's products and customer stories, while effectively managing logistics, timelines, and coordination across internal teams. The ideal candidate is a proactive communicator, a skilled problem solver, and a tech-savvy professional who thrives in a fast-paced, high-energy environment. As part of a growing team with an expanding portfolio of events, this position offers a unique opportunity to make a significant impact on our most important events of the year. 
   
 Responsibilities 
 
 Create and manage detailed project plans that outline strategies, objectives, deliverables, and timelines. 
 Plan and coordinate all logistical elements of events, including exhibit builds, catering, AV, branding, decor, and marketing materials. 
 Ensure that all events align with company priorities, meet and exceed specified goals, and adhere to budgetary guidelines for operational efficiency. 
 Collaborate with marketing, product, communications, and creative teams to identify and represent key stories and moments in activations, maintaining adherence to brand standards. 
 Oversee expo activations from initial planning to on-site execution, ensuring cross-functional team alignment and consistent delivery of high-quality experiences. 
 Conceptualize and produce immersive physical experiences that highlight Stripe products and customer stories, including thematic activations tailored for specific attendee segments. 
 Optimize expo elements for attendee flow, visibility, and engagement. 
 Support expo demo planning and staffing. 
 Intake information from internal teams to clarify goals and recommend budget-friendly activation solutions, setting clear expectations for scope and timelines. 
 Manage day-to-day interactions with production vendors, including layout planning, lighting, AV, electrical, and other logistical aspects. 
 Critically evaluate vendor proposals, advocating for customized solutions while identifying cost-effective materials and production methods that uphold quality. 
 Maintain clear production timelines, ensuring vendor progress aligns with project plans while managing risks and milestones. 
 Willingness to collaborate with vendors and cross-functional partners across Europe and Asia Pacific, effectively navigating different global time zones for event coordination. 
 Willingness to travel to support on-site execution for 4 or more events annually, including Stripe Sessions, Stripe Tour, and other regional initiatives. 
 
 Who you are 
 We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. 
 Minimum requirements 
 
 5+ years of professional experience in event management of large scale user conferences 
 Demonstrated leadership skills with strong ability to adapt to changing environments and prioritizing tasks 
 Proven experience in effective communications and relationship management 
 Able to work collaboratively, creatively and cross-functionally in a dynamic fast-moving work environment 
 Strong in developing processes and automating where possible; thinking at scale 
 Superior organizational, project management, written and verbal communication skills 
 Demonstrate attention to detail and proven track record to manage multiple competing priorities simultaneously 
 Experience with managing multiple complex projects simultaneously and independently, multiple vendor contracts and relationships. 
 Data-driven mindset to focus on setting goals, measuring KPIs, communicating milestones along the way, and teaming with other stakeholders to showcase the outcomes of each event to stakeholders 
 
 Preferred qualifications   
 
 Experience with using Slack, Google drive, Figma and Asana  
 Event planning experience in global markets 
 Extensive knowledge of large event venues globally

title: Executive Briefing Manager
content:
Who we are 
 About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the team 
 The Global Executive Marketing team creates experiences for the executive audience that build advocacy and trust, strengthen relationships, and generate revenue. We understand the executive audience and create content and experiences to programmatically reach them, helping Stripe attract, grow, and retain our most strategic users. The team is composed of three functional groups - 1/ Executive Briefings, 2/ Executive Programs and 3/ Executive Content - that work together to help users learn more about Stripe and discover new areas for collaboration. 
 What you’ll do 
 As a member of the Executive Briefing team, you will be on the ground floor defining the strategy of our program in EMEA and leading the evolution of our executive briefings in London, which includes our physical Center, as well as remote EBC experiences. You’ll be collaborating with Stripe executives, sales teams, product management and engineering teams across geographies to ensure continuous program innovation and enhanced offerings that allow our team to scale in support of the rapid growth in global commerce. 
 You will: 
 
 Evolve our executive briefing motion in London:  You will be the main PoC for our executive briefings in London and will lead the charge to advance our strategy. You will build upon the program's foundation and evolve our offerings to exceed the expectations of our customers and key internal stakeholders. 
 Elevate the in-person briefing experience : You will be the face of our executive briefing center (EBC) in our office in London. You will work with workspace and account teams to continuously elevate the experience we are delivering to executives that visit us in the space.  
 Build a trusted relationship with go-to-market teams.  You’ll serve as a primary contact and partner to sales teams, provide guidance on executive briefings and manage priorities/expectations at every stage of the customer lifecycle. 
 Design the customization of high-quality executive engagements.  You are customer-obsessed and listen closely to our customers to understand their unique challenges. You connect with expert Stripe teams to collaborate and deliver inspiring narratives within our executive engagements. You will customize agendas and ensure briefings run effectively in virtual and in-person settings. 
 Share customer feedback with sales and product teams : You’ll become an expert in the needs of customers and the expectations across the industry, documenting feedback that will influence product strategy and sales priorities. 
 
   
 Who you are 
 We’re looking for someone who will be a great addition to our growing team. It’s not expected that any single candidate would have expertise across all of the areas highlighted below but we’re looking for candidates that are particularly strong in a few areas, and have some interest and capabilities in others. 
 Minimum Requirements: 
 
 BA/BS degree (in lieu of degree, 4 years of relevant work experience) 
 6+ years of executive briefing, consulting or marketing experience, preferably in B2B scenarios. Experience in executive engagement programs, strategic partnerships, sales or sales enablement is a plus 
 Strong cross-functional collaboration skills with Leadership, Sales, Product, and Marketing teams to produce user-centric engagements 
 Excellent problem-solving, critical thinking, written and verbal communication, and analytical skills 
 Efficient project management experience. You have a strong track record of driving simultaneous projects and multiple initiatives 
 High energy and infectiously optimistic attitude. Works well under pressure, meets tight deadlines and doesn’t get phased by rapidly changing environments 
 
 Preferred Qualifications: 
 
 Experience in the payments, financial technology, or adjacent industries or marketing FinTech solutions or marketing to FinTech companies 
 Experience managing multiple vendors including catering, swag suppliers, etc 
 Experience at a highly dynamic, high-growth tech company or consulting firm 
 Experience working as part of an international team, split across multiple time zones.

title: Executive Engagement Manager
content:
Who we are 
 About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the team 
 The Executive Programs team sits within Global Executive Marketing and is responsible for designing and delivering Stripe’s most strategic executive-facing programs and engagements. Our work connects Stripe’s leadership team with senior leaders across our customer and partner ecosystem through thoughtfully curated, high-impact experiences. 
   
 The team owns flagship programs including Stripe’s annual Executive Summit, Customer Advisory Boards, and Stripe’s presence at global forums such as the World Economic Forum’s Annual Meeting in Davos. Executive Programs partners closely with stakeholders across the company to ensure these programs are cohesive, intentional, and aligned to Stripe’s most important business priorities. 
 What you’ll do 
 As a Global Executive Engagement Manager, you will help evolve Stripe’s executive engagement motion into a centralized, scalable engine that supports the company’s most strategic priorities. You will play a key role in shaping how Stripe plans, prepares for, and executes executive engagements across global events, market visits, and field activations. 
   
 This role requires strong executive presence, sound judgment, and the ability to operate comfortably at senior levels of the organization. You will partner closely with leadership offices, Associate Business Partners (ABPs), Field Marketing, Product Marketing, and Sales—owning the strategy, planning, and preparation for executive engagements, while working through partners to execute logistics and follow-through. 
 Responsibilities 
 Executive engagement strategy & governance 
 
 Help define and operationalize Stripe’s executive engagement model across global events, market visits, and field activations 
 Partner with leadership offices and cross-functional stakeholders to prioritize executive participation based on company goals, must-win accounts, and strategic themes 
 Maintain a holistic view of executive engagement activity to ensure clarity of ownership, balanced workloads, and alignment with broader business priorities 
 
 Executive engagement planning & prioritization 
 
 Own and evolve Stripe’s executive engagement intake and prioritization approach, ensuring requests are evaluated consistently and strategically 
 Drive thoughtful peer-to-peer executive mapping aligned to executive interests, expertise, and business objectives 
 Synthesize executive engagement requests across Marketing and Sales partners into a cohesive, high-impact engagement plan 
 
 Executive preparation & experience excellence 
 
 Set the standard for executive preparation across all engagements, ensuring leaders are equipped with clear context, objectives, and insights 
 Orchestrate the development and quality control of executive briefing materials in partnership with account teams and sales leadership 
 Define and standardize preparation best practices for global events, market visits, and field engagements to ensure a consistent executive experience 
 
 Program operations & tooling 
 
 Define and maintain SOPs for executive engagement preparation, debriefs, and action tracking 
 Leverage spreadsheets, CRM systems, and internal tools to track executive engagement activity, manage prioritization, and support reporting 
 Identify opportunities to improve workflows, documentation, and tooling to enable scale without sacrificing quality 
 
 Cross-functional and regional partnership 
 
 Partner closely with Associate Business Partners (ABPs), who own executive scheduling and logistics, while this role owns the  who, why, and what  of executive engagement 
 Collaborate with Field Marketing, Events, Sales, and regional executive marketers to deliver consistent executive experiences globally Support localization needs for global engagement, including regional context, cultural considerations, and market-specific playbooks 
 
 Who you are 
 We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. 
 Minimum requirements 
 
 7+ years of experience in executive programs, executive marketing, strategic events, field marketing, or a related role 
 Demonstrated executive presence and executive-level communication skills, with the ability to confidently engage, influence, and advise senior leaders and their offices 
 Proven ability to distill complex business context into clear, concise executive narratives and briefing materials 
 High level of comfort working in spreadsheets and CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce), including managing data sets, tracking engagement activity, and producing insights to inform planning and prioritization 
 Strong program management skills with the ability to operate across multiple stakeholders, timelines, and regions 
 Excellent judgment, attention to detail, and ability to perform effectively in high-stakes, fast-moving environments 
 
 Preferred qualifications 
 
 Experience operating at global scale across EMEA, APAC, and North America 
 Background in B2B, enterprise technology, or financial services 
 Experience designing governance, prioritization, or operating models for executive or leadership engagement 
 Familiarity with account-based engagement and field-driven GTM motions

title: Field Marketing Manager, Northern Europe/MEA
content:
Who we are 
 About Stripe 
 Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world's largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the team 
 The Stripe EMEA Marketing team is a dynamic and rapidly growing group dedicated to transforming customers into enthusiastic advocates for Stripe. Collaborating closely with sales, we focus on accelerating the path to purchase and enhancing product adoption among our user base. Our regional marketing efforts involve crafting and executing tailored marketing plans that resonate with local audiences, as well as spearheading our most significant conferences and sponsorships that drive go-to-market (GTM) success and business growth. Join us and be part of a culture that celebrates teamwork, innovates boldly, and makes a meaningful impact. 
 What you’ll do 
 We’re looking for a strategic, entrepreneurial Field Marketing Manager to lead our marketing efforts across the Northern Europe and MEA cluster (encompassing Benelux, Nordics, Israel, CEE, and Middle East and Africa).  
 This is a unique, senior individual contributor role for a builder who views marketing through a business development lens. Because these markets are at various stages of maturity, you will thrive at the intersection of strategy and execution—balancing the need to build brand awareness with the tactical requirements of establishing product-market fit. You will work hand-in-hand with a highly collaborative Sales team to identify market opportunities, navigate local nuances, and translate global priorities into programs that resonate across a diverse geographic footprint. 
 Responsibilities 
 
 Define and execute the field marketing strategy across the Northern Europe and MEA cluster, with a focus on established enterprises, digital native businesses, and SaaS platforms. 
 Partner closely with Sales to align account plans and develop marketing strategies that generate pipeline and enhance product adoption in emerging markets. 
 Partner with Demand Generation to define and execute full-funnel marketing plans including digital marketing, demand generation campaigns, sales prospecting programs, and Account-Based Marketing (ABM). 
 Oversee the regional events strategy and execution, incorporating third-party and Stripe-hosted events in in-person, and virtual formats. 
 Partner with Product Marketing to identify, scope, and produce or localize compelling content and marketing assets for diverse local audiences. 
 Own key business and revenue metrics across the sales funnel (pipeline) and customer growth, continually optimizing for efficiency and scale. 
 Be creative in finding new ways to advise a diverse range of personas about the value Stripe brings to businesses in markets with evolving product-market fit.  
 
 Who you are 
 We’re looking for an experienced, creative, and results-oriented leader who understands the diverse needs of businesses and can effectively collaborate with sales leaders to translate business needs into engagement opportunities. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. 
 Minimum requirements 
 
 7+ years of relevant B2B marketing experience in the Enterprise Technology market across multiple customer segments. 
 Proven track record of defining and executing field marketing programs that drive new customer acquisition and pipeline generation. 
 Exceptional communication skills, including being able to convey complex topics with simplicity to diverse regional audiences. 
 Strategic mindset with strong analytical capabilities; comfortable interpreting data to inform decision-making. 
 Experience collaborating closely with Sales teams as a strategic partner, establishing trust and driving joint planning. 
 Adaptability to manage multiple complex projects at once across a vast and diverse geographical region.  
 
 Preferred qualifications 
 
 A startup mentality with a bias to action and the ability to flex in a fast-paced industry and work environment. 
 Experience in the payments, financial technology, or adjacent industries, or marketing FinTech solutions or to FinTech companies. 
 Understanding of the payments, financial technology, and/or ecommerce industries and partner ecosystems. 
 Demonstrated success delivering marketing programs with and through consulting and technology partners.

title: Finance and Strategy Partner
location: Chicago, San Francisco, Seattle, New York City
content:
Who we are   
 About Stripe

Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. 
 About the team 
 The Finance & Strategy team is an integral thought partner to Stripe’s core functional leaders.  We bring understanding and predictability to Stripe’s bottom line financials.  We work cross functionally across many surfaces at Stripe and help steward Stripe’s resource investments to improve the durability of our long term financial performance. 
 What you’ll do 
 We're looking for a Finance & Strategy Partner to join our growing team. This individual will help us with the forecasting budgeting and reporting for various departments accurately and in a timely manner and deliver improvements in our integrated financial systems with business partners, financial colleagues, and engineers. 
 Responsibilities 
 
 Partner closely with business leaders to drive successful outcomes 
 Leverage your financial background to support and influence key strategic and business decisions 
 Be your business teams’ financial partner for analysis and evaluation of strategic projects and initiatives (e.g. foundational investments or business model shifts) 
 Design and provide analytical rigor to measure efficiency and ROI of investments in headcount and non-headcount expenses 
 Drive reporting, process and discipline for finance routines to shape and influence decision-making (e.g. budget, forecast, monthly reviews, long-range plans) 
 Build and own models for in-depth analyses, as well as ensure their quality, timeliness, and accuracy 
 Define metrics and leading indicators of business performance 
 Partner with cross functional teams, such as Data Science, to develop reporting dashboards, forecast 
 Drive continuous process improvement, standardization, simplification and reporting enhancements. 
 
 Who you are 
 We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. 
 Minimum requirements 
 
 7-9 years of relevant experience in strategic finance or FP&A  
 A keen analytical eye and experience building integrated models from scratch (in Excel or other planning applications) 
 Experience driving and executing on finance processes (e.g. budget, forecast reviews, negotiations) 
 A proven ability to build strong collaborative working relationships with business partners 
 A proven ability to be a self starter capable of driving business results without significant supervision 
 The ability to maintain high performance under tight timelines and a “roll up your sleeves” mindset to get any job done 
 The ability to bring structure to ambiguous areas of opportunity and thrive in an unstructured, fast-moving environment 
 Excellent communication and organizational skills, both written and verbal. 
 Excellent problem-solving skills and demonstrated ability to work independently, analyze problems and data sets to make complex investigation decisions 
 Strong knowledge of GSuite tools, Salesforce and MS-Office products and experience working with/analyzing large data sets. 
 Independently extract, analyze and evaluate information from various data sources through proficiency in SQL 
 Demonstrated experience partnering with cross-functional stakeholders 
 Demonstrated history of taking on various types of challenging projects and producing results 
 Solution-oriented mindset with enthusiasm for establishing best practices 
 Self-disciplined, diligent, proactive and detail oriented 
 
 You will also likely have (not required): 
 
 Experience working at a high-growth technology or financial services company 
 Experience managing headcount and operating expenses 
 Experience supporting Go-to-Market, Money Management, and/or Corporate Finance 
 Oracle, Anaplan, Tableau or other equivalent application experience 
 Experience with Python or a willingness to learn 
 Experience in data visualization and dashboarding
